## Summary

Detect and surface **Stellar network mismatch** when users connect Freighter/xBull with a different network than `NEXT_PUBLIC_STELLAR_NETWORK` — a common cause of silent auth failures (#11).

---

## Problem

ZCore dapp reads `NEXT_PUBLIC_STELLAR_NETWORK=testnet` but the user's wallet may be on mainnet (or vice versa). SEP-53 signatures use network-specific passphrases. Users see generic "Invalid wallet signature" (#11) without understanding the root cause.

---

## Proposed implementation

**File:** `Front/lib/wallet-kit.ts`

1. After wallet connect, read wallet network via Wallets Kit API (or Freighter `getNetwork()`)
2. Compare to `NEXT_PUBLIC_STELLAR_NETWORK`
3. If mismatch → throw typed error `NetworkMismatchError`

**UI:** `Front/components/wallet-auth-card.tsx`

```tsx
{error?.code === 'NETWORK_MISMATCH' && (
  <Alert variant="destructive">
    Tu wallet está en <strong>{walletNetwork}</strong> pero la dapp espera <strong>{expectedNetwork}</strong>.
    Cambia la red en Freighter e intenta de nuevo.
  </Alert>
)}
```

**Optional:** `Front/components/network-badge.tsx` — show live connected network vs expected

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Network checked before signing auth challenge
- [ ] Clear error message with both networks named
- [ ] No API call made if network mismatched (save roundtrip)
- [ ] Works with Freighter on testnet (primary wallet)
- [ ] Document in README troubleshooting table

---

## How to verify

1. Set dapp to testnet, connect Freighter on mainnet
2. Attempt register → see network mismatch error (not 401)

---

## Related

- #11 wallet auth failures
- `Front/components/network-badge.tsx` (existing)
- SEP-53 network passphrase docs

---

## Out of scope

- Auto-switch wallet network (wallet-dependent)
- Server-side network validation of signatures
